---
title: My dynamic report
output: html_fragment
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE)

```

```{css}

body{
  font-family: "Segoe UI", "Helvetica", "Arial", sans-serif;
  color: black;
  line-height: 1.6;
  margin: 20px;
}

h1{
  font-size: 32px;
  color: black;
}

h2{
  font-size: 24px;
  color: black;
}

h3{
  font-size: 20px;
  color: black;
}

p{
  font-size: 16px;
}

img{
  margin-top: 15px;
  margin-bottom: 15px;
}

.dynamic-text{
  color: black;
  font-weight: bold;
}

```

## Observing Phone Use Among Bus Passengers

```{r data_prep}

# Load necessary libraries for data manipulation and visualization
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(tidyverse)) # Load the tidyverse package

# Read the CSV data from the provided Google Sheets link
logged_data <- readr::read_csv("https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vTeLDMHtbbSEKTXmlMHI_Xx56G79uUAATt1yyA4Q4eS5xRcV92sRXacFiX0nF8PNBGX_dTS03ihS4Kv/pub?gid=1547770&single=true&output=csv")

# Rename columns to simpler, R-friendly names for easier reference
latest_data <- logged_data %>%
  rename(
    Date = Date.of.observation,
    Time = Time.of.observation,
    Phone_Users = How.many.passengers.are.using.their.phones.,
    Total_Passengers = How.many.passengers.can.you.see.on.the.bus.,
    Unusual_Observations = Any.unusual.observations.or.notes.
  ) %>%
  mutate(Phone_Percent = ifelse(
    Total_Passengers > 0, 
    (Phone_Users / Total_Passengers) * 100,
    NA
  ))

# Convert the Time column to a factor so that each observation has its own bar in the plots
latest_data$Time <- factor(latest_data$Time, levels = unique(latest_data$Time))


```

# Summary statistics

```{r summary, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}

# Summary statistics for amount of people using phones
avg_users <- mean(latest_data$Phone_Users, na.rm = TRUE) # Average number of passengers using phones

comment1 <- paste0("On average, about ", round(avg_users), " passengers on the bus were using their phones.")
cat(comment1, "\n")

max_users <- max(latest_data$Phone_Users, na.rm = TRUE) # Maximum number of passengers using phones

comment2 <- paste0("The maximum number of passengers observed using phones on the bus was ", max_users, ".")
cat(comment2, "\n")

# Calculate summary statistics percentage of people using phones
avg_percent <- mean(latest_data$Phone_Percent, na.rm = TRUE) # Average phone usage percent

comment3 <- paste0("On average, about ", round(avg_percent, 1), "% of passengers on the bus were using their phones.")
cat(comment3, "\n")

max_percent <- max(latest_data$Phone_Percent, na.rm = TRUE) # Maximum phone usage percent

comment4 <- paste0("The maximum percentage of passengers observed using phones on the bus was ", round(max_percent, 1), "%.")
cat(comment4, "\n")

```

# Graphs

```{r plot1, echo=FALSE}

# Bar Chart 1: Number of Phone Users by Time
p1 <- latest_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Time, y = Phone_Users)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") + # Use geom_bar with stat="identity" to plot y-values directly
  labs(title = "Number of Phone Users by Time",
       x = "Time of Observation",
       y = "Number of Phone Users",
       caption = "Shows how many passengers were using phones at each observation time.") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) # Rotate x-axis labels for easier readability

print(p1)

```

```{r plot2, echo=FALSE}
# Bar Chart 2: Percentage of Passengers Using Phones by Time
p2 <- latest_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Time, y = Phone_Percent)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") + # Use geom_bar with stat="identity" to plot y-values directly
  labs(title = "Percentage of Passengers Using Phones by Time",
       x = "Time of Observation",
       y = "Percentage of passengers using phones (%)",
       caption = "Shows the percentage of passengers using phones at each observation time.") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) # Rotate x-axis labels for easier readability

print(p2)

```
